task: Launch-quality advanced task: Wildfire Smoke HVAC Protection Plan. Reality context: a hospital campus needs HVAC settings for forecast smoke events. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=pm25_outdoor_ug_m3, metric2=filter_merv, metric3=recirc_pct, metric4=indoor_pm25_est; records: R1 pm25_outdoor_ug_m3=187 filter_merv=77.69 recirc_pct=38.1 indoor_pm25_est=39.43; R2 pm25_outdoor_ug_m3=221 filter_merv=63.1 recirc_pct=69.6 indoor_pm25_est=74.84; R3 pm25_outdoor_ug_m3=173 filter_merv=16.19 recirc_pct=1.4 indoor_pm25_est=79.4; R4 pm25_outdoor_ug_m3=169 filter_merv=71.36 recirc_pct=30.3 indoor_pm25_est=41.86; limit_pm25_outdoor_ug_m3=105; limit_filter_merv=34.03; limit_recirc_pct=93.6; decision_budget_usd=182500; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="dataset documentation emphasized provenance, exclusions, and reproducibility". Rules and constraints: Separate safety/compliance blockers from economic optimizations; blockers override ROI, while nonblockers are ranked by expected value per unit cost. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: choose filtration and operations plan; rank records by technical and economic impact, run the stated sensitivity check, and recommend hold, release, retest, or mitigation. Deliver smoke_hvac_plan.xlsx + facilities_memo.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
